description_long: This object is a futuristic, angular airplane with a low-profile fuselage and sharply swept wings that extend outward and slightly downward. The body is predominantly dark gray with subtle paneling and geometric surface details, giving it a stealthy, military-inspired appearance. The wings are broad and flat, with no visible winglets, and the rear section features a compact, integrated tail assembly. The engines are mounted on the sides of the fuselage, partially embedded and streamlined into the wing roots. The cockpit is positioned forward, seamlessly blending into the fuselage with a low, tinted canopy. The overall design suggests a focus on aerodynamic efficiency and stealth, with minimal protrusions and a smooth, hard surface finish.
description: A sleek, dark gray airplane with sharply swept wings, a low-profile fuselage, and a forward cockpit, featuring side-mounted engines and a compact tail assembly.
description_view_0: Side view showing the sharply swept wings, low-profile fuselage, and partially embedded engine detail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide, angular wingspan and the forward placement of the cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Opposite side view revealing the symmetrical wing and engine configuration, as well as the streamlined body.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the compact tail assembly and the broad, flat wings extending outward.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and no visible winglets; wings extend outward and slightly downward with a flat profile.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the wing roots on either side of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Compact conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and integrated horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear, partially visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a low-profile, tinted canopy integrated into the fuselage.
